As a result, James was only charged with misdemeanor counts of retail theft, resisting obstructing an officer, and he was released a few days later on a $0 signature bond.

Over the next five months, James was arrested once more and faced new charges for three separate incidents, including attempted battery threats to an officer, strangulation and suffocation, resisting arrest, and disorderly conduct.

Regarding the Menard's theft incident, James was found guilty due to a no contest plea to both counts and was sentenced to seven days in county jail, which had already been served by the time of his sentence hearing.
